Actually, `*.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` only matches exactly one subdomain level below `elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local`. 

So `es-http.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` would match, but `es-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` wouldn't because it has an extra level. apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: false

    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate

    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - elasticsearch-0
      - elasticsearch-1
      - elasticsearch-2
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      securityContext:
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: sysctl
          image: busybox
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
        - name: permissions
          image: busybox
          command: ["chown", "-R", "1000:0", "/usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          securityContext:
            runAsUser: 0
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          ports:
            - name: https
              containerPort: 9200
            - name: transport
              containerPort: 9300
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - bash
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -sf \
                    --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt \
                    -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    "https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&timeout=5s"
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 20
            timeoutSeconds: 10
          livenessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 9300
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 15
            failureThreshold: 10
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
        - name: data
          emptyDir: {}

content.thinking:
The pod is running but it can't resolve `elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local` because that pod doesn't exist yet (StatefulSet creates pods sequentially). The pod `elasticsearch-0` is waiting for the readiness probe to pass, but the readiness probe is waiting for `yellow` cluster health which requires at least 2 of 3 nodes.

Wait, the StatefulSet creates pods in order: 0, 1, 2. Pod 0 must be Ready before pod 1 is created, and pod 1 must be Ready before pod 2 is created. 

This creates a deadlock—pod 0 can't reach yellow status without the other nodes, but those nodes won't start until pod 0 is ready. I need to change the readiness probe to check if the local Elasticsearch node is running instead of waiting for cluster health, maybe by just verifying port 9200 is responding.

With security enabled, I could use a curl command that authenticates with the elastic user and checks if the node itself is up. This should succeed as soon as the node starts, regardless of cluster formation. Though that assumes the elastic password is already configured, which might require the cluster to be bootstrapped first.

I'm realizing there's a fundamental bootstrapping problem here: StatefulSets create pods sequentially and only mark them Ready once they pass health checks, but Elasticsearch needs multiple nodes to form a quorum. So the first pod can't become Ready without the others, but the others won't start until the first is Ready.

The cleanest solution is to switch the StatefulSet to parallel pod creation instead of sequential. This way all three nodes start simultaneously and can form a cluster together, bypassing the readiness check bottleneck. Updating the StatefulSet with the parallel pod management policy.

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0

    xpack.security.enabled: true
    xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: false

    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt

    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: true
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate

    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - elasticsearch-0.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-1.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
      - elasticsearch-2.es-headless.elasticsearch.svc.cluster.local
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - elasticsearch-0
      - elasticsearch-1
      - elasticsearch-2
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
      targetPort: 9300
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      securityContext:
        fsGroup: 1000
      initContainers:
        - name: sysctl
          image: busybox
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
        - name: permissions
          image: busybox
          command: ["chown", "-R", "1000:0", "/usr/share/elasticsearch/data"]
          volumeMounts:
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          securityContext:
            runAsUser: 0
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          ports:
            - name: https
              containerPort: 9200
            - name: transport
              containerPort: 9300
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - bash
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -sf \
                    --cacert /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt \
                    -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    "https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health?wait_for_status=yellow&timeout=5s"
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 20
            timeoutSeconds: 10
          livenessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 9300
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 15
            failureThreshold: 10
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
        - name: data
          emptyDir: {}
